input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue with keyboard handling in .NET 10. In .NET 9, they used `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize`, but it no longer works in .NET 10. Let me provide guidance based on what I know about .NET MAUI's evolution.

In .NET MAUI (.NET 8+), the `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust` was already being deprecated/changed. By .NET 10, the Android `Resize` mode for soft input was removed from Android itself (Android 15+ deprecated `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_RESIZE`).


The modern approach involves using the `WindowInsets` API for edge-to-edge display handling on Android, or falling back to `SOFT_INPUT_ADJUST_PAN` if needed. In newer .NET MAUI versions, `KeyboardAutoManagerScroll` or manual inset handling are the recommended solutions for managing keyboard overlap.
